sources.notes: Construct an array satisfying interval min/mex constraints.
Official tutorial classifies positions by whether they are inside min intervals and mex intervals.
Maspy explains the four position classes and the periodic i mod k filling for mex-only positions.

annotation.summary: Construct an array satisfying all range minimum and mex constraints.
annotation.constraints: n,q are large; the construction must be near-linear after interval marking.
annotation.core_idea: Classify each position by whether it lies inside a minimum constraint interval and/or a mex constraint interval. Min-only positions can safely be k, both-type positions can be k+1, and irrelevant positions are arbitrary. The hard class is mex-only; filling these positions cyclically as 0,1,...,k-1 ensures every long enough mex interval can see a full residue set.
annotation.complexity: O((n+q) log n) or O(n+q) with difference arrays.
tricks: periodic fill by i mod k
use large sentinel values for min-covered positions

solution_variants.name: coverage-classification-construction
solution_variants.summary: Mark min/mex interval coverage, assign fixed sentinel values by class, and fill mex-only cells periodically.

tags.evidence: The task is solved by explicitly assigning values according to interval coverage classes.
The main work is marking interval coverage and assigning the four position classes without off-by-one mistakes.
Positions are classified by overlap of min-interval coverage and mex-interval coverage.
